page: Amazon Web Services c7i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific single-threaded and text-processing workloads. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1912.0 and a text processing score of 2399.0, both placing in the strong tier. Gzip single-threaded compression reached 32.2 GB/sec, also a strong result. Multi-core performance is characterized as average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28316.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14186.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 2443.1 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 137.9 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations. These hardware features contribute to top-tier text generation speeds of 380.26 tokens/second during LLM inference with a small 135M model, and strong prompt processing speeds of 206.92 tokens/second with a medium 7B model. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 48.15 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, though the lack of local storage requires network-attached storage configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1912.0 and a gzip compression speed of 32.2 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, enabling top-tier text generation speeds of 380.26 tokens/second for small LLM models. This server is suited for compute-heavy tasks, including software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong single-core performance and advanced instruction support.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations.
Strong single-core performance and high-throughput static web serving.
No local storage or GPU hardware, utilizing a 12 Gbps baseline network interface.
Well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
